deferred_smith_review_queue: Choose whether the next declared work should be repair, rc-literal reproduction, broader audit, or no further work.
If repair is declared, choose preferred enforcement surface candidate: driver post-completion predicate or explicit Link gate policy wiring.
narrowly_proven: D1 is carried by two QA lanes: the enforcement asymmetry is located in support/operator/driver.py predicate/branch logic and in brick/comparison.py/write_observation.py recording-only comparison facts. A mixed diff containing at least one allowed path and at least one outside-allowed non-forbidden path is not caught by either current post-completion hold predicate.

D2 is carried at disposition level: the isolated A_plus_B reproduction did not trigger the hold predicates, while the B_only control did trigger the fake-landing hold predicate. The literal shell/process rc values are not present in the curated carried summaries.

D3 is carried: two repair candidates were named by the investigation and accepted by QA as in-scope investigation output: add a driver post-completion predicate for any path outside allowed_paths, or route observed_paths_outside_declared_scope into an explicit gate. No repair implementation was in scope.
The carried evidence supports investigation closure only: source mutation, project residue, and git commit were not part of the declared task and no source diff is claimed by this closure.
not_proven: Literal reproduction process return-code numbers for D2 are not present in the carried curated summaries; the disposition booleans and hold/no-hold outcomes are carried instead.
Whether the proposed repair candidates would close the gray zone without regressions is not proven because implementation and checker changes were outside this Building scope.
Whether other Buildings beyond the named evidence have landed similar mixed-scope diffs is not proven.

Carried attack-QA evidence from work/step-outputs/task-statement-70600589359b-node-code-attack-qa-attempt-1/step-output.json: QA independently re-derived the driver predicate behavior, built fresh isolated /tmp fixtures, and checked production Building evidence including t1s2v4-bothgates-0704a and commit 706b5922.

Comparison evidence carried by both QA lanes: brick/comparison.py computes observed_paths_outside_declared_scope, and support/operator/write_observation.py records the bucket, but carried grep observations found no support/operator consumer that turns that bucket into a post-completion hold.

Reproduction evidence carried by attack-QA: A_plus_B fixture, with one allowed path plus one outside-allowed and non-forbidden path, produced _write_need_complete_without_scoped_diff_for_plan=False and _write_need_complete_with_forbidden_diff_for_plan=False, so no hold fired; B_only control produced fake-landing hold behavior.

Production evidence carried by attack-QA: commit 706b5922 for task-statement-b0eac2272663-node includes support/checkers/check_profile.py outside that Building's declared allowed_paths and outside forbidden_paths, with no forbidden/fake-landing hold record reported in the Building evidence tree.
